subject: Biology
title: Predator–Prey Arms Races
description: A quick, practical guide to Predator–Prey Arms Races.

hook.text: Cheetahs evolved to run 110 km/h. Gazelles evolved to zigzag at 80 km/h. Neither can afford to lose.

buildup.text: Predators evolve better weapons. Prey evolve better defenses. It never stops.

discovery.text: Rough-skinned newts produce deadly tetrodotoxin. Garter snakes evolved resistance to eat them anyway.

twist.text: Each escalation by one side drives a counter-adaptation in the other—the Red Queen hypothesis.

climax.text: This endless competition is one of the most powerful engines of evolutionary change.

punchline.text: Run faster, bite harder, hide better. Or go extinct.

quiz.question: What is the Red Queen hypothesis?
options: Species must keep adapting just to survive against co-evolving rivals
Red coloring signals danger in nature
